title: Orthochilus ovalis
taxonomy: Orthochilus ovalis is classified as a species of orchid, belonging to the family Orchidaceae. It is a type of terrestrial orchid, which grows in various habitats around the world. Within the Orchidaceae family, Orthochilus ovalis is a member of the subfamily Vanilloideae and the tribe Vanilleae. The species is further classified as a member of the genus Orthochilus, which consists of several other species of orchids. The classification of Orthochilus ovalis reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history as a distinct species within the orchid family.

intro: Orthochilus ovalis is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the oval-leaved orchid. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Orthochilus ovalis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Orthochilus ovalis is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory infections, colds, and flu.
